commonProblems.h2: Mitsubishi Xpander Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Mitsubishi Xpander's 4A91 1.5L MIVEC petrol engine dominates UK replacement enquiries, with timing chain tensioner wear, head gasket failure, and oil pump issues driving most searches. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address the original failure point - plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty. Use this section to compare repair vs replacement costs for your specific variant.

problems.h4: Timing Chain Tensioner Failure - Why Mitsubishi Xpander 4A91 Engines Rattle
problems.affectedModels: Mitsubishi Xpander GLX, GLS, Exceed, Ultimate, Cross (2017-Present, 4A91 MIVEC)
problems.rootCause: OEM tensioner part MB524273 loses hydraulic pressure, allowing chain slack and rattle on cold start.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace tensioner MB524273, inspect chain guides, reset timing
repairOptions.longevity: Viable only if caught early before chain stretch; prevents catastrophic failure

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace tensioner, chain, guides, sprockets; full timing service
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended if rattle present; extends engine life 40k+ miles

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt 4A91 with upgraded tensioner kit, bench-tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best value if engine has suffered chain jump or valve damage

problems.recommendation: On a Mitsubishi Xpander worth £8,000-£14,000, preventative tensioner replacement at 60k miles is cost-effective. If rattle is present or engine has jumped timing, a rebuilt 4A91 with upgraded components offers better long-term value than repeated repairs.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt 4A91 engine prices for your Mitsubishi Xpander

problems.h4: Head Gasket Failure - Mitsubishi Xpander 1.5 Overheating Issues
problems.rootCause: Gasket part MD981658 degrades under repeated overheating; coolant loss leads to combustion gas leakage.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove cylinder head, replace MD981658 gasket, machine head surface, refill coolant
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if block is undamaged; requires specialist machining

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt 4A91 with upgraded gasket set, pressure-tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended if block has warped or head is cracked; faster turnaround

problems.recommendation: If your Xpander has overheated and shows white smoke or coolant loss, get a compression test immediately. For engines with confirmed head gasket failure but no block damage, specialist gasket replacement is viable. If the block is warped, a rebuilt unit is more reliable and often similarly priced.
problems.cta: Get quotes for Mitsubishi Xpander head gasket replacement or rebuilt engine

problems.h4: Oil Pump Failure - Mitsubishi Xpander Oil Starvation Risk
problems.rootCause: OEM oil pump MD011449 bearing wear reduces pressure; leads to oil starvation and bearing seizure.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Drop sump, replace MD011449 pump, replace pickup screen, refill oil
repairOptions.longevity: Only viable if caught before bearing damage; requires early warning signs

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt 4A91 with upgraded oil pump, new bearings, bench-tested
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended if oil pressure warning appeared or knocking heard; prevents repeat failure

problems.recommendation: Oil pump failure on the 4A91 is often silent until damage occurs. If your Xpander shows low oil pressure or knocking, assume bearing damage has occurred. A rebuilt engine with upgraded pump and bearings is the only reliable solution - minor pump replacement risks repeat failure.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt 4A91 engine prices with upgraded oil pump for your Mitsubishi Xpander

problems.h4: Engine Knocking Sound - Mitsubishi Xpander TSB Issue
problems.rootCause: Subject of Mitsubishi Technical Service Letter; piston pin clearance and rod bearing wear cause knock under load.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Compression test, oil analysis, bore scope inspection to confirm knock source
repairOptions.longevity: Essential first step; determines if repair or replacement is viable

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt 4A91 with upgraded pistons, rods, bearings; bench-tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Only reliable solution for confirmed knock; prevents catastrophic seizure

problems.recommendation: Engine knocking on the 4A91 is not a "live-with-it" issue. If your Xpander knocks under acceleration, stop driving immediately and get a diagnostic. For confirmed knock, a rebuilt engine is the only safe, cost-effective solution - attempting partial repair risks total engine loss.
problems.cta: Get quotes for Mitsubishi Xpander 4A91 rebuilt engine with upgraded internals

problems.h4: Oil Consumption Fix - Mitsubishi Xpander Burning Oil
problems.rootCause: Piston ring wear and valve stem seal hardening cause oil burn; common on high-mileage 4A91 units.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove valve cover, replace stem seals via compressed air method; no head removal
repairOptions.longevity: Viable for mild oil consumption; reduces burn by 50-70%

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove engine, replace piston rings, hone bores, reassemble; major labour
repairOptions.longevity: Viable for moderate consumption; requires specialist machining

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt 4A91 with upgraded rings and seals, bench-tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best value for severe consumption or engines over 100k miles

problems.recommendation: For mild oil consumption (1L per 3,000 miles), valve stem seal replacement offers good value. For severe consumption or engines over 100k miles, a rebuilt 4A91 with upgraded components is more reliable and often similarly priced to major ring replacement.
problems.cta: Compare Mitsubishi Xpander oil consumption repair vs rebuilt engine prices

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Mitsubishi Xpander
finalCta.paragraph: The Mitsubishi Xpander's 4A91 engine is robust when maintained, but known failure points like timing chain tensioners, head gaskets, and oil pumps can lead to costly repairs. EngineMarket makes it simple to compare prices from trusted UK Mitsubishi Xpander specialists who understand grey import engines. Every rebuilt engine includes upgraded components addressing original failure points, plus nationwide delivery and a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty. Compare prices and get back on the road without the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Mitsubishi Xpander Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Mitsubishi Xpander and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineSizes.h2: Mitsubishi Xpander Engines by Size - Find the Right Displacement for Your Variant
engineSizes.intro: Every displacement fitted to the Mitsubishi Xpander is listed below, with compatible engine codes, production years and common failure notes to help you identify the right replacement.

items.title: Mitsubishi Xpander 1.3L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 1.3L 4A90 was the entry-level petrol option for early Mitsubishi Xpander commercial and fleet variants. Sharing architecture with the more common 1.5L 4A91, it offered adequate power for light-duty use but is now less commonly searched for replacement in the UK grey import market.
commonFailurePoints: Valve stem seal wear causing oil consumption at high mileage
items.cta: Compare Mitsubishi Xpander 1.3L petrol engine prices

items.title: Mitsubishi Xpander 1.5L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 1.5L 4A91 MIVEC is the backbone of the Mitsubishi Xpander range, fitted to GLX, GLS, Exceed, Ultimate, Cross and Rockford Fosgate variants from 2017-present. Its aluminium construction and MIVEC variable valve timing deliver balanced performance and fuel economy for this budget MPV.
items.cta: Compare Mitsubishi Xpander 1.5L petrol engine prices

items.title: Mitsubishi Xpander 1.6L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 1.6L 3A92 was an early option for GLS and Exceed variants, offering slightly more torque for load-carrying use. Less common than the 1.5L 4A91 in UK imports, it shares similar maintenance requirements and failure patterns.
commonFailurePoints: ignition coil pack failure causing misfire
items.cta: Compare Mitsubishi Xpander 1.6L petrol engine prices

items.title: Mitsubishi Xpander 1.8L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 1.8L 4B10 was the top-spec petrol option for Ultimate and Cross variants, providing enhanced torque for towing and motorway use. Rare in UK grey imports but sought after by owners needing maximum power from the GA chassis platform.
commonFailurePoints: thermostat housing crack on early production units
items.cta: Compare Mitsubishi Xpander 1.8L petrol engine prices

items.title: Mitsubishi Xpander 1.5L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 1.5L 4N13 diesel was a rare EU-export option for Mitsubishi Xpander GLS and Ultimate variants. Very limited UK availability as a grey import; owners should verify chassis compatibility before sourcing replacement engines.
commonFailurePoints: DPF regeneration issues on short-journey use
items.cta: Get quotes for Mitsubishi Xpander 1.5L diesel engines

items.title: Mitsubishi Xpander 1.8L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 1.8L 4N14 diesel was the higher-output option for commercial and load-carrying Xpander variants. Extremely rare in the UK grey import market; replacement engines typically require sourcing from European breakers.
commonFailurePoints: turbo oil seal leak at high mileage
items.cta: Get quotes for Mitsubishi Xpander 1.8L diesel engines

items.title: Mitsubishi Xpander Petrol Engines
items.description: The Mitsubishi Xpander is predominantly a petrol-powered MPV in the UK grey import market, with the 1.5L 4A91 MIVEC engine fitted to over 90% of variants.
items.descriptor: The Mitsubishi Xpander is predominantly a petrol-powered MPV in the UK grey import market, with the 1.5L 4A91 MIVEC engine fitted to over 90% of variants.
items.familiesLabel: Common Mitsubishi Xpander Petrol Engine Families
families: 4A91 - 1.5L MIVEC, primary engine for GLX/GLS/Ultimate/Cross variants, timing chain tensioner risk
4A90 - 1.3L entry-level for commercial variants, shares architecture with 4A91
3A92 - 1.6L early GLS/Exceed option, slightly more torque for load carrying
4B10 - 1.8L top-spec for Ultimate/Cross, enhanced torque for towing

Timing chain tensioner MB524273 wear between 60k-90k miles
Head gasket MD981658 failure after overheating events
High UK replacement demand on GLS, Ultimate and Cross variants

items.descriptor: Diesel Mitsubishi Xpander variants (4N13 1.5L and 4N14 1.8L) were EU-export only and are extremely rare as UK grey imports; most "diesel Xpander" searches relate to misinformation.

foundIn: EU-spec GA chassis (2018-2020) - 4N13, 4N14 (not UK official imports)
knownFor: Better fuel economy than petrol variants in long-distance use
EGR and DPF issues on short-journey UK driving patterns
Very limited UK supplier availability for replacement engines
Higher replacement cost due to rarity and import complexity
typicalModels: Mitsubishi Xpander Commercial LWB Diesel 1.8 (EU import)
importantNotes: Diesel Xpander variants were never officially sold in the UK

engineYears.h2: Mitsubishi Xpander Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This section covers all major engine changes across the Mitsubishi Xpander's production timeline from 2017-present, organised so grey import owners can quickly identify their 4A91 engine and compare rebuilt prices from UK specialists.

years.description: The Mitsubishi Xpander launched on the GA chassis with the 4A91 1.5L MIVEC petrol engine as the primary powertrain across GLX, GLS and early Exceed variants.
keyChanges: GA chassis platform debut for Mitsubishi MPV segment
GLX and GLS trim levels launched with 105hp output
Early production units used pre-facelift timing components
knownFor: Robust aluminium block design with MIVEC variable valve timing
Early tensioner MB524273 units prone to wear before 70k miles
Head gasket MD981658 specification later revised for improved durability
High search volume from UK grey import owners identifying engine codes
years.cta: Get quotes for 2017-2019 Mitsubishi Xpander replacement engines
years.ticker: Mitsubishi Xpander 2017 engine replacement • 4A91 timing chain rattle fix • GLS 1.5 engine price UK

years.description: The facelift Mitsubishi Xpander introduced updated 4A91 MIVEC engines with revised timing components and improved cooling for enhanced reliability.
keyChanges: Revised 4A91 MIVEC with updated tensioner and gasket specifications
Cross variant launched with raised suspension and black cladding
Ultimate and Rockford Fosgate trims added premium features
Improved water pump and thermostat housing design
mainEngines: 4A91 MIVEC - 1.5L petrol updated (GLS / Exceed / Ultimate / Cross)
knownFor: Updated timing components reduced early tensioner failure rates
Cross variant popular with UK grey import buyers seeking SUV styling
Oil pump MD011449 still requires monitoring beyond 80k miles
Strong demand for rebuilt 4A91 engines with upgraded internals
years.ticker: Xpander Cross 2021 engine • 4A91 MIVEC rebuilt price • Ultimate 1.5 engine replacement cost

years.description: Latest Mitsubishi Xpander models feature refined 4A91 MIVEC engines with further component upgrades and enhanced emissions compliance for global markets.
keyChanges: Further refined 4A91 MIVEC with improved piston ring design
Enhanced ECU mapping for better fuel economy and emissions
S-AWC variant introduced with all-wheel-drive capability
Updated service intervals and maintenance specifications
knownFor: Latest 4A91 revisions address earlier oil consumption concerns
S-AWC variant adds complexity but retains 4A91 core architecture
UK grey import supply still dominated by 2017-2023 models
Rebuilt engines with latest spec components offer best longevity
years.cta: Enquire about Mitsubishi Xpander 2024-2025 engine availability

items.question: What engines fit a Mitsubishi Xpander?
items.answer: The primary engine for Mitsubishi Xpander variants is the 4A91 1.5L MIVEC petrol unit. Other compatible codes include 4A90 (1.3L), 3A92 (1.6L), and 4B10 (1.8L) depending on variant and year.
keyPoints: 4A91 1.5L MIVEC fits GLX, GLS, Exceed, Ultimate, Cross variants
Verify engine code via registration check before ordering replacement
GA chassis mounting points are consistent across petrol variants
Diesel engines (4N13/4N14) require chassis compatibility verification
items.cta: Get quotes for compatible Mitsubishi Xpander 4A91 engines

items.question: How much does it cost to replace a Mitsubishi Xpander engine?
items.answer: Rebuilt Mitsubishi Xpander 4A91 engines typically cost £1,400-£2,900 supply-only in the UK. Supply & fit adds £400-£800 depending on location and variant complexity.
keyPoints: Rebuilt 4A91 prices range £1,400-£2,900 based on variant and supplier
Supply & fit service adds £400-£800 for labour and installation
Used engines start around £900 but carry shorter warranty terms
All EngineMarket quotes include minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty
items.cta: Compare Mitsubishi Xpander engine replacement costs today

items.question: Does the UK have diesel Mitsubishi Xpander engines?
items.answer: Diesel Mitsubishi Xpander variants (4N13/4N14) were EU-export only and are extremely rare as UK grey imports. Most UK Xpanders use the 4A91 1.5L petrol engine.
keyPoints: Over 90% of UK grey import Xpanders use 4A91 1.5L petrol engines
Diesel variants (4N13 1.5L, 4N14 1.8L) were EU-specification only
Verify your engine code via registration check before sourcing diesel replacement
Specialist knowledge required for diesel EGR and DPF systems
items.cta: Check if your Mitsubishi Xpander is petrol or diesel now

items.question: What causes Mitsubishi Xpander 4A91 timing chain failure?
items.answer: Timing chain tensioner part MB524273 loses hydraulic pressure on Mitsubishi Xpander 4A91 engines, causing chain slack and rattle that can lead to valve damage if ignored.
keyPoints: OEM tensioner MB524273 typically fails between 60k-90k miles
Cold-start rattle is the primary warning symptom
Rebuilt 4A91 engines include upgraded tensioner kits as standard
Preventative replacement costs £450-£850 specialist price
items.cta: Compare rebuilt 4A91 engines with upgraded timing components

items.question: Do you warranty grey import Mitsubishi Xpander engines?
items.answer: Yes, every engine supplied through EngineMarket for Mitsubishi Xpander grey imports includes a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty from UK specialists.
keyPoints: 12-month unlimited mileage warranty standard on rebuilt units
Warranty covers parts and labour when supply & fit is booked
Specialists understand grey import documentation requirements
Confirm exact warranty terms with your chosen supplier before purchase
items.cta: Get warranty-backed Mitsubishi Xpander engine quotes now

items.question: Is the Mitsubishi Xpander Cross engine different?
items.answer: The Mitsubishi Xpander Cross uses the same 4A91 1.5L MIVEC engine as standard variants, with identical mounting points and compatibility for replacement engines.
keyPoints: Cross variant uses standard 4A91 1.5L MIVEC engine code
Raised suspension does not affect engine fitment or compatibility
Rebuilt 4A91 engines fit Cross variants without modification
Cross-specific styling does not impact engine replacement process
items.cta: Compare Mitsubishi Xpander Cross engine replacement prices

items.question: How long does a Mitsubishi Xpander 4A91 engine last?
items.answer: With proper maintenance, Mitsubishi Xpander 4A91 engines can exceed 150,000 miles. Key to longevity is addressing timing chain tensioner and oil pump issues before catastrophic failure.
keyPoints: Well-maintained 4A91 engines regularly reach 150k+ miles
Timing chain tensioner replacement at 60k miles prevents major failure
Regular oil changes with correct specification critical for oil pump longevity
Rebuilt engines with upgraded components offer like-new reliability
items.cta: Get quotes for long-life Mitsubishi Xpander 4A91 rebuilt engines

items.question: Can I fit a Mitsubishi Xpander engine myself?
items.answer: While technically possible, fitting a Mitsubishi Xpander engine requires specialist knowledge of GA chassis mounting, ECU reset procedures, and grey import compliance - professional supply & fit is recommended.
keyPoints: GA chassis engine removal requires specific tools and procedures
ECU reset and immobiliser pairing needs specialist diagnostic equipment
Grey import compliance documentation may be required post-fitment
EngineMarket network specialists offer supply & fit to eliminate risk
items.cta: Find Mitsubishi Xpander engine supply & fit specialists UK

items.question: What causes Mitsubishi Xpander 4A91 head gasket failure?
items.answer: Head gasket part MD981658 on Mitsubishi Xpander 4A91 engines can fail after overheating events, allowing coolant loss and combustion gas leakage between cylinders.
keyPoints: Overheating is the primary cause of MD981658 gasket failure
White smoke from exhaust and coolant loss are key symptoms
Rebuilt 4A91 engines include upgraded gasket sets as standard
Compression testing recommended before deciding repair vs replacement
items.cta: Compare Mitsubishi Xpander head gasket repair vs rebuilt engine costs
